name: Keyboard Shortcuts
summary: Standardized keyboard interaction management enabling consistent shortcut handling across shadow boundaries and OS-specific layouts.

name: Composite Widget
summary: Focus management and navigation protocol for complex widgets like Grids, Trees, and Menus, implementing the Roving Tabindex pattern.

name: Droplist
summary: Accessible dropdown menu supporting keyboard navigation, typeahead, and ARIA roles.

name: Validation
summary: Standard behaviors for native-like form validation in custom elements.

name: Resource Loader
summary: Structural directive using comment-based virtual elements to handle async state without polluting the DOM.

fetchAPI.pattern: fetch() with AbortController for cancellable requests
fetchAPI.adoption: All loader operations use AbortSignal for cancellation; superseded navigations abort automatically

abortController.adoption: Loaders receive signal param; AbortSignal.timeout() for built-in timeout support

cacheAPI.adoption: Custom Cache sub-standard provides cache policy; runtime can delegate to Cache API

httpCacheControl.adoption: withStaleWhileRevalidate trait mirrors this pattern at the UI level

ariaBusy.pattern: aria-busy for announcing loading state to assistive technology
ariaBusy.adoption: All strategy traits set aria-busy='true' on the loading region during async operations

progressElement.pattern: <progress> element for native determinate progress indication
progressElement.adoption: withDeterminate trait renders native <progress> with value/max attributes

resource-load-start.description: Fired when an async loading operation begins

resource-load-end.description: Fired when an async loading operation completes successfully

resource-load-error.description: Fired when an async loading operation fails

resource-state-change.description: Fired on any state machine transition (idle, pending, loading, success, empty, error, stale)

events.rationale: Typed event classes registered via provider key. Enables listenForScoped discrimination when multiple loaders coexist (e.g., router loader + inline data grid loader firing load-end simultaneously).

virtualElement.description: Uses comment-based markers instead of wrapper elements
virtualElement.rationale: Preserves parent-child CSS layout (Grid/Flex). No extra <div> between parent and content.

traitComposition.description: Loading behaviors are composed via mixin traits, not subclassing
traitComposition.rationale: Strategy (soft/hard/replacement/optimistic) and progress (determinate/indeterminate) are orthogonal dimensions. Mixins compose better than a class hierarchy.

intentDriven.description: Block does not decide loading UX — it reads LoaderIntent from the injector chain
intentDriven.rationale: Decouples the loading consumer from the loading UX. Apps declare intent once; all consumers (router, forms, grids) adapt consistently.

stateMachine.description: 7-state machine governing the full async lifecycle
stateMachine.rationale: pending vs loading distinction enables debounced timing (no flash for fast ops). empty is distinct from success for empty-state UX. stale enables stale-while-revalidate pattern.

registryFirst.description: Loading templates (skeletons, error cards, empty states) are defined in CustomResourceRegistry, not inline
registryFirst.rationale: Ensures consistent loading visuals across the application. Inline templates create visual inconsistency.

tanstackQuery.notes: Stale-while-revalidate model with hierarchical cache invalidation by key

swr.notes: Lightweight SWR pattern from Vercel; minimal API with built-in deduplication

apolloClient.notes: Link middleware pattern is the inspiration for Custom Middleware sub-standard

reactSuspense.notes: Declarative loading boundaries; similar concept to our virtual element approach

remix.notes: Route-level data fetching with progressive enhancement; inspiration for Router integration

traits.name: withOptimistic
traits.description: Non-blocking — shows predicted outcome immediately, reverts on failure

traits.name: withSoftBlocking
traits.description: Input-blocking — applies aria-disabled and pointer-events:none while content remains visible

traits.name: withHardBlocking
traits.description: Visual-blocking — obscures or overlays content with a backdrop

traits.name: withReplacement
traits.description: Content-blocking — replaces view with placeholder (skeleton, spinner)

traits.name: withDeterminate
traits.description: Renders measurable progress (0-100%) as a progress bar or percentage

traits.name: withIndeterminate
traits.description: Renders activity indication (spinner, pulse, shimmer) without measurable progress

traits.name: withSkeleton
traits.description: Placeholder content matching page structure for initial loads

traits.name: withStaleWhileRevalidate
traits.description: Keeps previous content visible (dimmed/stale) while refreshing

name: Resource Action
summary: Manages async mutation lifecycle (POST/PUT/DELETE) — busy state on triggers, double-submit prevention, optimistic updates with automatic rollback on error.

fetchAPI.adoption: Action operations delegate to Custom Clients for transport; AbortController for cancellation

invokerCommands.adoption: Action triggers can use Invoker Commands for mutation invocation

ariaDisabled.pattern: aria-disabled for non-focusable disable pattern during pending state
ariaDisabled.adoption: withBusyTrigger applies aria-disabled + pointer-events:none during mutation

resource-action-end.description: Fired when a mutation completes successfully

resource-action-revert.description: Fired when an optimistic update is rolled back due to error

events.rationale: Typed event classes enable listenForScoped discrimination between multiple concurrent mutations (e.g., save + delete actions on the same form).

mutationVsQuery.description: Resource Action handles mutations (writes); Resource Loader handles queries (reads)
mutationVsQuery.rationale: Reads and writes have fundamentally different UX patterns. Reads show loading states in-place; writes show busy states on triggers. Splitting them avoids feature bloat.

doubleSubmitPrevention.description: Automatically prevents double submission by disabling triggers during pending state
doubleSubmitPrevention.rationale: Every mutation UI needs double-submit prevention. Building it into the directive avoids repetitive boilerplate.

optimisticRevert.description: Optimistic updates are automatically reverted on error
optimisticRevert.rationale: Optimistic UI is the happy path; revert-on-error is the safety net. The directive manages both sides of the lifecycle.

virtualElement.description: Uses comment-based markers like Resource Loader for layout preservation
virtualElement.rationale: Same reasoning as Resource Loader — no wrapper divs that would break Grid/Flex parent-child relationships.

tanstackQuery.notes: Mutation lifecycle with optimistic update/rollback and automatic cache invalidation

apolloClient.notes: GraphQL mutations with optimistic cache updates

remix.notes: Progressive enhancement forms with server actions

swr.notes: Separated mutation from query with manual trigger

traits.name: withBusyTrigger
traits.description: Applies busy state to the triggering element during mutation (width locking, aria-disabled, indicator)

traits.name: withOptimisticMutation
traits.description: Applies predicted result immediately, reverts on error

traits.name: withFeedbackOnComplete
traits.description: Dispatches toast/notification on success or error

name: For Each
summary: Structural directive for rendering lists of items based on an array.

name: Handler Expression Parser
summary: Parser for function-call style event handler expressions. Extends CustomAttributeParser for registry integration.

magicVariables.description: Magic variables are prefixed with $ to distinguish them from user-defined state

variables.$event: The native DOM Event object passed to the handler
variables.$element: The element the event handler is attached to
variables.$target: The event.target - the element that triggered the event

magicVariables.rationale: Using $ is common in Vue, Svelte, and other frameworks. It creates a clear visual distinction between framework-provided values and user data.

namedContexts.description: Named contexts are prefixed with @ to access scoped data from parent elements

examples.@state: Explicit access to the default state context

namedContexts.rationale: The @ prefix is distinct from $ and provides clear scoping. Similar to Angular's template reference variables but for contexts.

defaultState.description: Bare identifiers resolve to the default state context
defaultState.rationale: Most expressions reference state, so bare identifiers reduce verbosity. @state.count and count are equivalent.

contextShadowing.description: Controls behavior when a context name shadows another in nested scopes
contextShadowing.rationale: Shadowing can cause subtle bugs. Default to warning to alert developers while not breaking during development.

forEachNaming.description: The 'as' keyword names the loop context
forEachNaming.rationale: Matches PHP/Blade/many template languages. Intuitive for developers familiar with foreach loops.

name: Event Behaviors (on:*)
summary: Declarative event-to-action mapping using expression syntax with context resolution.

configuration.getHandlers: Required. Function returning handler registry for an element
configuration.getContexts: Optional. Function returning named contexts Map
configuration.getState: Optional. Function returning default state object
configuration.parserName: Optional. Name of parser in CustomAttributeParserRegistry (default: 'handler')

parserResolution.description: HandlerExpressionParser is resolved from CustomAttributeParserRegistry in the injector chain
parserResolution.error: MissingParserError thrown if no parser found in registry

designRationale: Parser registry allows different DOM subtrees to use different parser configurations. Parsers extend CustomAttributeParser for consistency with the attribute parsing system.

name: Broadcast
summary: Declarative DOM-to-JS bridge for provider-scoped broadcast events. Fills the platform gap between targeted Invoker Commands and global event dispatch.

invokerCommands.gap: No broadcast equivalent — commands are always 1-to-1 via element ID

commandEvent.pattern: CommandEvent is constructable but designed for declarative button triggers, not programmatic broadcast

customEvent.pattern: CustomEvent + dispatchEvent is the current broadcast mechanism
customEvent.gap: Globally-scoped string names — no discrimination, no scoping

naming.rationale: Broadcast clearly communicates 'everyone, do this' intent. Mirrors the event taxonomy from Web Events research. 'dispatch' is too generic (could mean targeted). 'emit' is framework jargon (Node.js EventEmitter, Vue $emit).

dispatchTarget.rationale: Broadcast commands are global by definition. Subtree-scoped broadcast is a separate unsolved problem (DOM events only bubble up, not down). Keep the block simple and single-purpose.

triggerEvent.configurable: broadcast-on attribute for other triggers (e.g., change, submit)
triggerEvent.rationale: Buttons are the primary broadcast trigger. Other elements may need different trigger events.

providerResolution.description: Resolves event class from the injector scope of the host element, constructs an instance, dispatches on document
providerResolution.rationale: The host element's scope determines which event class to use. This allows different parts of the app to broadcast different event classes for the same provider key.

detailSource.description: Optional broadcast-detail attribute for static detail, or broadcast-from attribute referencing a store/context for dynamic detail
detailSource.rationale: Broadcast commands often carry payload (e.g., theme name, locale code). Static JSON covers simple cases; store reference covers reactive cases.

name: Trusted HTML
summary: Structural directive enforcing Trusted Types policies on dynamic HTML content via comment-based virtual elements.

name: Trusted HTML Behavior
summary: Attribute-based behavior that enforces Trusted Types policies on an element's innerHTML mutations.

name: View
summary: Foundation for all show/hide/render patterns. Provides a shared engine and specialized blocks (behaviors + directives) that higher-level components build on.

ViewEngine.description: Shared core: hidden modes, toggle events, transitions, exclusive groups. Not a block — imported by blocks that need it.

ViewBehavior.description: Imperative show/hide via Invoker Commands. Element always in DOM.

ViewShowBehavior.description: Expression-bound reactive visibility. Element always in DOM.

ViewIfDirective.description: Structural conditional rendering. Nothing in DOM when false.

ViewSwitchDirective.description: Multi-branch structural rendering. One branch at a time.

exclusiveAccordion.adoption: ViewEngine uses name attribute for exclusive view groups

hiddenUntilFound.adoption: ViewEngine hidden mode option for searchable hidden content

popoverEvents.pattern: beforetoggle (cancelable) and toggle events with oldState/newState
popoverEvents.adoption: ViewEngine fires same events with same detail shape

contentVisibility.pattern: content-visibility: hidden for performance, auto for lazy rendering
contentVisibility.adoption: ViewEngine default hidden mode (performant, keeps a11y tree)

inertAttribute.pattern: inert makes content non-interactive but visible
inertAttribute.adoption: ViewEngine hidden mode for preview/disabled states

viewTransitionsAPI.adoption: ViewEngine wraps show/hide in view transitions when opted in

domParts.pattern: Low-level API for marking DOM positions for updates
domParts.adoption: Directives use comment markers today, ready to adopt DOM Parts when available

nomenclature.rationale: "View" is framework-agnostic, implies visual display, and pairs naturally with router terminology. Dropped 'Conditional' from name — View is the foundation, not one specific pattern.

architecture.description: View separates HOW to hide/show (engine) from WHEN to hide/show (blocks)
architecture.engine: ViewEngine handles hidden modes, toggle events, transitions, exclusive groups
architecture.blocks: Each block solves a different DOM-presence scenario: always present (Behavior) vs stamped on demand (Directive)
architecture.rationale: Higher-level components (Tabs, Router, Accordion) pick the block that fits their DOM model, or use the engine directly. No reimplementation of show/hide mechanics.

blockSelection.description: The right block depends on what exists in the DOM when content is inactive

criteria.domPresence: Does the content always exist in HTML, or is it created on demand?
criteria.accessibility: Should hidden content remain in a11y tree or be findable via Ctrl+F?
criteria.statePreservation: Should component state survive hide/show cycles?

blockSelection.rationale: Making the DOM-presence question explicit prevents misuse. view:show and view:if look similar but have fundamentally different DOM models.

exclusiveGroups.description: Views with same name attribute are mutually exclusive (only one visible)
exclusiveGroups.rationale: Mirrors <details name="..."> pattern from HTML standard. Familiar, consistent with platform direction.

triggers.rationale: Align with Invoker Commands API (Baseline Jan 2026). Custom commands use -- prefix per spec.

hiddenModes.description: Multiple ways to hide content with different tradeoffs

modes.content-visibility:hidden: Default. Hidden, skipped for rendering, stays in a11y tree
modes.display:none: Fully hidden, no layout cost, no accessibility

hiddenModes.rationale: content-visibility provides best performance while maintaining accessibility tree presence. Directives don't use hidden modes by default (they remove DOM), only when using keep-alive.

events.rationale: Consistency with platform. Developers familiar with popover events. Typed event classes enable provider-based discrimination via Web Events — a parent can distinguish a View toggle from a Popover toggle using listenForScoped.
events.collisionRisk: High — 'beforetoggle' and 'toggle' are shared with Popover API and <details>. Without scoped events, a parent listening for 'toggle' would receive events from both views and popovers.

statePreservation.description: Caches hidden content state instead of destroying it. Only relevant for directives (behaviors preserve state naturally).
statePreservation.rationale: Inspired by Vue KeepAlive. When using keep-alive, directives delegate hide/show of cached content to ViewEngine.

transitions.description: Opt-in to native browser View Transitions when switching content
transitions.rationale: View Transitions API is baseline in all browsers (2024). Native > library.

name: Tabs
summary: Accessible tab interface component built on View (ViewBehavior for panel switching), providing tab triggers, panels, and ARIA semantics.

exclusiveGroups.usage: Panels share name attribute for mutual exclusivity

openUITabs.status: Following Open UI research for future native alignment

architecture.description: Tabs is a thin UI layer over View. Uses ViewBehavior for panel switching (panels always in DOM) and ViewEngine for hidden modes and toggle events.

responsibilities.tabs: ARIA roles, keyboard navigation, active styling
responsibilities.viewBehavior: Panel show/hide via commands and exclusive groups

architecture.rationale: Tabs panels are always present in DOM (behavior pattern), not stamped on demand (directive pattern). ViewBehavior is the right primitive.

options.name: Attribute-based (recommended)
options.rationale: Works with any markup, progressive enhancement

syntax.chosen: Attribute-based for core, custom element as optional wrapper

activationMode.automatic: Tab activates on focus (arrow keys switch tabs)
activationMode.manual: Tab activates on Enter/Space (arrow keys only move focus)
activationMode.rationale: Automatic is more accessible, manual allows preview before commit

orientation.description: Affects keyboard navigation direction and ARIA orientation
orientation.rationale: Vertical tabs are common for settings pages, mobile navigation

stateSync.urlHash: Optional sync with URL hash for bookmarkable tabs
stateSync.rationale: Users expect tab state to persist on refresh or when sharing URLs

tab-change.description: Fired when the active tab changes

tab-close.description: Fired when a closeable tab is dismissed

events.rationale: Typed event classes registered via provider key. Module-prefixed names ('tab-change') reduce accidental collision; provider-based instanceof discrimination provides structural guarantee via Web Events.

traits.name: withCloseableTabs
traits.description: Adds close buttons to tabs for dismissible panels

traits.name: withReorderableTabs
traits.description: Enables drag-to-reorder for tab triggers

traits.name: withOverflowTabs
traits.description: Handles tab overflow via scrolling or dropdown menu when tabs exceed available width

traits.name: withLazyTabs
traits.description: Renders panel content only on first activation for performance

name: Router
summary: Declarative client-side routing built on Navigation API, URLPattern, and ViewEngine.

viewIntegration.description: <route-view> uses ViewEngine directly — it does its own structural DOM stamping in-place (or into named outlets) but delegates hide/show mechanics to ViewEngine.

uses.ViewEngine: Hidden modes for keep-alive cached routes inside <route-view> (or named outlets), toggle events (beforetoggle/toggle), View Transitions API integration
uses.ViewBehavior: Not used — router content doesn't exist until a route matches, no pre-existing elements to attach behavior to
uses.ViewIfDirective: Not used — router has its own URL-based matching, not expression-based conditionals
uses.ViewSwitchDirective: Not used — router manages its own outlet stamping

viewIntegration.rationale: Route content shouldn't exist in DOM until the URL matches. No placeholders, no ghost elements. <route-view> does structural work in-place; ViewEngine provides shared show/hide mechanics.

navigationAPI.pattern: Modern API replacing History API with events, traversal info, and interception
navigationAPI.adoption: Primary navigation mechanism, fallback to History API for Safari <17.2

urlPatternAPI.pattern: URLPattern for route pattern matching with named groups
urlPatternAPI.adoption: Use for all route pattern matching, enables :param and * wildcards

viewTransitionsAPI.adoption: Opt-in via route:transition attribute for smooth navigation

speculationRulesAPI.adoption: Generate speculation rules from route definitions for prefetching

historyAPI.adoption: Fallback only for browsers without Navigation API

hybridArchitecture.description: Router uses a hybrid pattern: components for structural boundaries, behaviors for enhancing existing elements, plain HTML attributes for declarative metadata. Primary rendering is in-place inside <route-view>.

components.RouteViewElement: Custom element (<route-view>). Orchestrator that owns child templates, listens to Navigation API, and stamps matched content in-place as its own children. The router renders where you define the routes.
components.RouteOutletElement: Custom element (<route-outlet>). Named auxiliary views only — for cases where a route renders content to a different area outside the <route-view>.

behaviors.RouteLinkBehavior: CustomAttribute on <a> (route:link). Must preserve native anchor semantics.
behaviors.RoutePrefetchBehavior: CustomAttribute on <a> (route:prefetch). Metadata paired with route:link.

plainAttributes.description: route, route:guard, route:guard:leave, route:loader, route:error, route:outlet on <template> elements are plain HTML attributes read by <route-view> via querySelectorAll. No classes or registry entries needed.
plainAttributes.rationale: Eliminates RouteAttribute, RouteGuardAttribute, RouteLoaderAttribute, RouteErrorAttribute classes. The config component owns template parsing.

hybridArchitecture.rationale: In-place rendering is the simplest model — the router renders where you define the routes. Named outlets exist only for auxiliary views (sidebar, header, footer). Links must stay as <a> elements for accessibility.

naming.rationale: Noun-based prefix consistent with view: and on: conventions. Component tag names use route- prefix. Template attributes are plain HTML read by the view component — no registry needed.

nomenclature.outlet: Named insertion point for routed content (from React Router/Angular)
nomenclature.rationale: Terms from React Router and Angular Router, widely understood

patternSyntax.description: Route patterns follow URLPattern API syntax
patternSyntax.rationale: URLPattern is a web standard, no need for custom syntax

outlets.primary: <route-view> renders matched content in-place — no separate outlet needed for the primary path
outlets.named: Named outlets (<route-outlet name="...">)  for auxiliary views only (sidebars, headers, footers)
outlets.rationale: In-place rendering is the simplest model. Named outlets exist for the special case where a route needs to render content outside the <route-view>.

links.rationale: Declarative links enable pre-rendering and active state styling

route.providedTo: Stamped content inside <route-view> (or named <route-outlet>), set on the element's injector
route.shape: RouteContext { params, data, error, path, query, hash, state }

loaderIntent.description: Declares how route loading states affect the UI. Router resolves this from the injector chain and selects matching Resource Loader traits. Falls back to soft blocking (aria-busy + CSS) when not provided.

contextProviders.rationale: All router data flows through customContexts, consistent with the framework's @ prefix convention. Guards and loaders are named functions resolved from their respective contexts, not magic globals. The loaderIntent context is consumed (not provided) by the router — it reads the app's declared loading UX profile.

guards.description: Plain functions (RouteGuardFn), not class instances. Provided via customContexts:routeGuard, resolved by name from route:guard and route:guard:leave attribute values.
guards.noAbstractClass: The Navigation API's navigate event already provides the guard mechanism — event.intercept() to proceed, event.preventDefault() to cancel, event.signal to abort. An abstract RouteGuard class would duplicate platform-level control. Guard functions are stateless decision points; the injector/context system handles DI via closures.

navigationApiMapping.event.intercept({ handler }): Guard returns true, proceed with navigation pipeline
navigationApiMapping.event.signal: Passed to loaders for automatic abort on navigation supersede

guards.rationale: Functional guards follow Angular v15+ direction (away from class-based CanActivate). Simpler, composable, testable. Navigation API is the single integration point — Router wraps it, not duplicates it.

loaders.description: Named async functions provided via customContexts:routeLoader, resolved by name from route:loader attribute value
loaders.integration: Router resolves customContexts:loaderIntent from the injector chain. The intent's strategy dimension selects which Resource Loader trait controls the loading UX (soft, hard, replacement, optimistic). Per-route overrides via route:loader-strategy, route:loader-context, route:loader-timing attributes.
loaders.rationale: Inspired by React Router loaders, Remix, TanStack Router. Context-based resolution keeps loaders injectable and testable. The Loader Intent bridge means apps declare loading UX once; all consumers (router, forms, data grids) adapt consistently.

errorHandling.description: Error boundary for route loading failures
errorHandling.rationale: Clean error UI without try-catch in user code

lazyLoading.description: Code-split route components, load on navigation

scrollRestoration.rationale: Navigation API provides native scroll restoration

prefetching.rationale: Predictive loading for instant navigation feel

route-change.description: Fired when the active route changes

route-load-start.description: Fired when a route loader begins fetching data

route-load-end.description: Fired when a route loader finishes fetching data

route-error.description: Fired when a route loader fails

events.rationale: Module-prefixed names ('route-change') reduce collision risk. Typed event classes with provider keys enable listenForScoped discrimination for apps embedding multiple routers or combining routers with other blocks that fire change/error events.

sveltekit.notes: File-based but concepts translate to declarative

name: SimpleStore
summary: Concrete implementation of CustomStore with built-in listener management, dot-notation paths, and derived state hooks.

dotNotationPaths.description: Get and set nested values using dot notation (e.g., 'user.name')
dotNotationPaths.rationale: Simplifies working with nested state without manual object traversal

onBeforeNotify.description: Optional hook called before listeners are notified
onBeforeNotify.rationale: Enables derived state calculations (e.g., computed totals) to be updated atomically before any subscriber sees the change

autoCreateIntermediate.description: setItem automatically creates intermediate objects for nested paths
autoCreateIntermediate.rationale: Reduces boilerplate when setting deeply nested values

name: Prefetch Behavior
summary: Attribute-based behavior that implements the Prefetch Intent by detecting user conditions via browser APIs and triggering resource prefetching.

traits.name: withViewportPrefetch
traits.description: Triggers prefetch when link enters the viewport via IntersectionObserver

traits.name: withHoverPrefetch
traits.description: Triggers prefetch on pointer hover (>200ms debounce) or focus

traits.name: withInteractionPrefetch
traits.description: Triggers prefetch at pointer down (moment of intent)

speculationRules.adoption: Aligns eagerness model (conservative/moderate/eager/immediate) with our intent levels; uses browser-native prefetch when available

intersectionObserver.pattern: Observes element visibility relative to viewport/ancestor
intersectionObserver.adoption: withViewportPrefetch uses IntersectionObserver with configurable rootMargin to trigger prefetch before element is fully visible

navigatorConnection.adoption: Suppresses prefetch when saveData is true or effectiveType is 'slow-2g'/'2g'

batteryStatus.pattern: Battery Status API: navigator.getBattery() for charging/level
batteryStatus.adoption: Suppresses viewport-level prefetch when battery < 20% and not charging

linkRelPrefetch.adoption: When the prefetched resource is a navigation, injects <link rel="prefetch"> into <head> for browser-native fetching

events.name: prefetch-triggered
events.description: Fired when a prefetch is about to start. Cancel to suppress.

events.name: prefetch-suppressed
events.description: Fired when a prefetch is suppressed due to user conditions (saveData, low battery, slow connection).

events.name: prefetch-complete
events.description: Fired when prefetched data is stored and ready for instant navigation.

remix.notes: Three eagerness levels on Link component; 'intent' = hover+focus, 'render' = immediate, 'viewport' = IntersectionObserver

nextjs.notes: Automatic viewport prefetch on all Links; uses IntersectionObserver internally

quicklink.notes: Library by Google: prefetches in-viewport links during idle time, respects saveData and effectiveType

instantPage.notes: Library: prefetches on hover (65ms debounce) or touchstart; minimal overhead approach

speculationRulesAPI.notes: Chrome's native declarative prefetch/prerender; our eagerness levels align with this model

conditionDetection.description: Maps Prefetch Intent user conditions to browser APIs: limited-data → saveData, low-power → Battery API, slow-connection → effectiveType
conditionDetection.rationale: The intent declares abstract conditions. This block maps them to concrete browser APIs with graceful degradation when APIs are unavailable.

eagernessToEvents.description: Maps eagerness levels to DOM events: viewport → IntersectionObserver, hover → pointerenter (>200ms), interaction → pointerdown
eagernessToEvents.rationale: Each eagerness level corresponds to a specific user gesture timing. The block translates the abstract intent into concrete event listeners.

suppressionPriority.description: Conditions are checked in priority order: saveData > low battery > slow connection. Any match suppresses.
suppressionPriority.rationale: User's explicit data-saving preference (saveData) should always win. Battery and connection are softer signals.

speculationRulesAlignment.description: Eagerness levels map to Speculation Rules API terms: viewport=moderate, hover=conservative, interaction=conservative, immediate=eager
speculationRulesAlignment.rationale: Aligning with the emerging browser standard ensures forward-compatible semantics and potential delegation to native prefetch.

name: Action Button
summary: Behavioral implementation of the Action Intent, handling visual priority rendering, busy state layout (width locking, visibility), and platform-aware group ordering.

traits.name: withBusyDisabled
traits.description: Disables the action during pending state (aria-disabled + pointer-events:none)

traits.name: withBusyInteractive
traits.description: Keeps the action interactive during pending state (reduced opacity indicator)

traits.name: withPlatformOrdering
traits.description: Reorders action groups based on OS conventions (macOS: primary last, Windows: primary first)

traits.name: withWidthLock
traits.description: Captures computed width before busy state, applies as inline min-width to prevent layout shift

invokerCommands.pattern: commandfor/command attributes for declarative action invocation
invokerCommands.adoption: Action buttons can use commandfor to target Resource Action elements; command attribute specifies the mutation

ariaDisabled.pattern: aria-disabled keeps element focusable but non-interactive
ariaDisabled.adoption: withBusyDisabled applies aria-disabled instead of HTML disabled, preserving tab order and screen reader announcements

ariaLive.pattern: aria-live regions for dynamic content announcements
ariaLive.adoption: Busy state changes announced via aria-live='polite' on the button's label region

cssContainment.pattern: CSS contain: layout for isolation from reflow
cssContainment.adoption: Action groups use contain: layout to prevent busy-state width changes from triggering ancestor reflow

materialUI.notes: Loading state with spinner replacement; icon positioning; size variants

radixUI.notes: Composable primitives; state exposed via data attributes for CSS styling

fluentUI.notes: Action button variants; split button for primary + secondary actions

headlessUI.notes: Headless approach: behavior without opinions on styling; closest to our intent model

layoutShiftPrevention.description: lockWidth: captures computed width before entering busy state, applies it as inline min-width
layoutShiftPrevention.rationale: Prevents layout reflow when label text changes (e.g., 'Save' → 'Saving...'). The intent declares the UX need; this block implements the CSS technique.

visibilityMapping.description: Maps intent busy state to CSS: disabled → aria-disabled + pointer-events:none, interactive → opacity:0.7
visibilityMapping.rationale: Translates the abstract 'interaction' field from ActionBusy into concrete accessibility attributes and CSS.

iconIntegration.description: Resolves icon name/size from Icon Intent, renders via icon provider
iconIntegration.rationale: Icons are a cross-cutting concern. The Action block delegates icon rendering to whatever icon system is provided via the Icon Intent.

ariaOverDisabled.description: Uses aria-disabled instead of HTML disabled attribute during busy state
ariaOverDisabled.rationale: HTML disabled removes the element from tab order and prevents screen reader interaction. aria-disabled keeps focus and announcements while preventing activation.

platformOrdering.description: macOS convention: primary action last (right). Windows convention: primary action first (left). Detects via navigator.platform or user configuration.
platformOrdering.rationale: Users have muscle memory for platform conventions. Getting this wrong forces cognitive overhead on every confirmation dialog.

name: Breakpoint Observer
summary: Runtime implementation of the Breakpoint Intent, mapping semantic step names to concrete CSS media queries or container queries and exposing reactive state.

traits.name: withMobileFirst
traits.description: Accumulative min-width queries — start small, add complexity as space grows

traits.name: withDesktopFirst
traits.description: Subtractive max-width queries — start large, remove features as space shrinks

traits.name: withStrictRange
traits.description: Isolated range queries — each tier is an independent bucket with no accumulation

traits.name: withContainerQueries
traits.description: Component-scoped breakpoints using CSS container queries instead of viewport media queries

unitMapping.description: Converts semantic steps to pixel/em/rem breakpoints based on application configuration
unitMapping.rationale: The intent declares abstract steps ('compact', 'medium', 'expanded'). This block maps them to concrete values (e.g., compact: 0-599px, medium: 600-904px).

queryAPI.description: Exposes matches(query) method for components to test current breakpoint: matches({ up: 'medium' }), matches({ only: 'compact' })
queryAPI.rationale: Components need to query the active breakpoint at runtime. This block provides the matchMedia/container query implementation.

containerQuerySupport.description: Supports both viewport-level (media queries) and component-level (container queries) breakpoints
containerQuerySupport.rationale: Modern layouts use container queries for component-scoped responsiveness. The block detects and uses the appropriate API.

name: Error Recovery
summary: Technical implementation of the Reliability Intent, handling retry strategies (exponential backoff, linear, immediate), HTTP status classification, and offline queuing.

traits.name: withAutomaticRetry
traits.description: Silent retry with configurable backoff (exponential, linear, immediate)

traits.name: withManualRetry
traits.description: Presents a 'Retry' affordance — user decides when to try again

traits.name: withFallbackSkeleton
traits.description: Shows structural placeholder implying content may load later

traits.name: withFallbackPrevious
traits.description: Shows stale data from last successful load, marked as outdated

traits.name: withCircuitBreaker
traits.description: Stops retrying after consecutive failures, enters open state with half-open probe

retryAfter.pattern: Server-specified retry timing via Retry-After header (seconds or HTTP-date)
retryAfter.adoption: When present on 429/503 responses, overrides configured backoff delay with server-specified wait time

httpStatusCodes.pattern: Semantic classification: 4xx client errors, 5xx server errors (RFC 9110)
httpStatusCodes.adoption: 4xx → terminal (no retry), 5xx → retryable with backoff, 429 → rate-limited with Retry-After

navigatorOnLine.pattern: navigator.onLine boolean + online/offline events on window
navigatorOnLine.adoption: Detects offline state to queue operations; listens for online event to trigger queued retries

backgroundSync.pattern: Service Worker Background Sync API — defers tasks until connectivity restored
backgroundSync.adoption: Progressive enhancement: if SW registered, queued mutations use Background Sync; otherwise falls back to online event polling

abortController.adoption: Each retry attempt gets a fresh signal; previous attempt aborted before retry dispatches

events.name: resource-retry
events.description: Fired before each retry attempt. Cancel to abort the retry sequence.

events.name: resource-retry-exhausted
events.description: All retry attempts exhausted. Block transitions to fallback UX.

events.name: resource-offline-queued
events.description: Operation queued for retry when connectivity is restored.

events.name: resource-circuit-open
events.description: Circuit breaker tripped open after consecutive failures.

tanstackQuery.notes: Configurable retry with custom delay functions; networkMode: 'online' pauses queries when offline

axiosRetry.notes: Interceptor-based retry with exponential backoff and configurable retry conditions

urql.notes: Exchange-based retry with jitter for distributed systems

swr.notes: Built-in error retry with exponential backoff; pauses when offline

apolloLink.notes: Link-based retry with configurable jitter to prevent thundering herd

retryStrategies.description: Implements three retry algorithms: immediate (0ms delay), linear (constant interval, e.g., 1s), exponential (doubling delay with optional jitter, e.g., 1s, 2s, 4s)
retryStrategies.rationale: The intent declares 'automatic' recovery. This block decides the backoff algorithm based on failure type. Jitter prevents thundering herd when many clients retry simultaneously.

httpStatusClassification.description: Maps HTTP status codes to recovery behavior: 4xx → terminal (no retry), 5xx → automatic retry with backoff, 429 → rate-limited retry respecting Retry-After header, offline → queue for background retry
httpStatusClassification.rationale: The intent declares abstract failure tolerance. This block provides the concrete mapping from HTTP semantics to recovery behavior.

maxRetries.description: Configurable maximum retry count (default: 3) with circuit breaker pattern
maxRetries.rationale: Prevents infinite retry loops. After max retries, the block transitions the UX to the intent's fallback content.

circuitBreaker.description: Three-state circuit (closed → open → half-open): closed allows requests, open fails fast after threshold, half-open probes with single request
circuitBreaker.rationale: Prevents wasted requests against a known-failing endpoint. Protects both the server (reduces load during outage) and the user (fast failure instead of timeout).

retryAfterRespect.description: Server-provided Retry-After header overrides configured backoff delay
retryAfterRespect.rationale: The server knows best when it will recover. Respecting Retry-After is both polite (reduces load) and effective (retries at the right time).

name: Workflow
summary: Multi-step process orchestration without URL changes — wizard-like flows with per-step validation, linear/branching progression, and state persistence. The non-route complement to Router's URL-based step navigation.

traits.name: withLinearProgression
traits.description: Steps must be completed in order; future steps are disabled until current passes validation

traits.name: withNonLinearProgression
traits.description: Steps can be visited in any order; all validated on final submit

traits.name: withBranchingLogic
traits.description: Step sequence adapts dynamically based on user input (progressive wizard)

traits.name: withGateValidation
traits.description: Validates current step fields via Constraint Validation API before allowing advancement

traits.name: withAutoSave
traits.description: Persists step data to sessionStorage/localStorage on each step change

traits.name: withReviewStep
traits.description: Final step displays summary of all collected data for confirmation

constraintValidation.pattern: checkValidity(), reportValidity(), setCustomValidity() for per-step field validation
constraintValidation.adoption: withGateValidation calls checkValidity() on the active step's fieldset before allowing advancement; hidden steps are automatically skipped by the browser

fieldset.pattern: <fieldset> + <legend> for semantic grouping; disabling a fieldset disables all children
fieldset.adoption: Each step is a <fieldset>; inactive steps get disabled attribute to prevent interaction and exclude from form validation

ariaCurrent.pattern: aria-current='step' marks the active step in a numbered indicator
ariaCurrent.adoption: Step indicator (progress rail) uses <ol> with aria-current='step' on the active <li>

invokerCommands.adoption: Next/Previous buttons use commandfor targeting the workflow element with command='--workflow-next' and command='--workflow-prev'

cssScrollSnap.pattern: CSS Scroll Snap for step transitions; scrollsnapchange event for JS hooks
cssScrollSnap.adoption: Optional trait: withSnapTransitions uses scroll-snap-type: x mandatory for horizontal step animation

dialog.pattern: <dialog> for modal wizard flows with native focus trapping
dialog.adoption: When workflow is rendered inside a <dialog>, focus management delegates to the browser's dialog focusing steps

events.name: workflow-step-before-change
events.description: Fired before step transition. Cancel to prevent advancement (e.g., failed validation).

events.name: workflow-step-change
events.description: Fired after step transition completes. Step content is now visible.

events.name: workflow-complete
events.description: Fired when user completes the final step. Cancel to prevent submission (e.g., final validation).

events.name: workflow-error
events.description: Fired when step validation fails. Provides the invalid fields and error messages.

angularCdkStepper.notes: Most complete headless stepper; linear mode with per-step FormGroup validation

muiStepper.notes: Display-only component; state management and validation are external

patternflyWizard.notes: Most fully specified wizard pattern; progressive wizard adds steps based on user input

stepperize.notes: Emerging standard for headless multi-step; ~1kB, framework-agnostic

reactHookForm.notes: Per-step trigger() enables field-level validation without full form submission

nonRouteWorkflow.description: Steps are state-based, not URL-based. No URL changes during step navigation.
nonRouteWorkflow.rationale: Router already handles URL-based multi-step flows. Workflow covers the complementary case: modal wizards, in-page flows, and short processes where URL bookmarking is unnecessary.

domPresence.description: All steps remain in DOM (behavior pattern). Inactive steps are hidden via View's exclusive group + disabled fieldset.
domPresence.rationale: State preservation across step navigation. Users expect form data to survive forward/back navigation. Consistent with Tabs pattern.

fieldsetPerStep.description: Each step wraps its content in a <fieldset> element. Inactive steps have disabled attribute.
fieldsetPerStep.rationale: Disabled fieldset prevents interaction with hidden step fields and automatically excludes them from form validation. Semantic grouping via <legend> for screen readers.

exclusiveGroupVisibility.description: Steps use View's name-based exclusive group. Showing a step auto-hides siblings.
exclusiveGroupVisibility.rationale: Reuses ViewEngine's proven exclusive group mechanism. Same pattern as Tabs panels and HTML <details name='...'>.

gateValidation.description: Per-step validation before advancement (gate model). Uses native Constraint Validation API.
gateValidation.rationale: Prevents invalid data from accumulating. Immediate feedback is the consensus best practice for complex flows (NN/G, PatternFly, Angular CDK).

invokerNavigation.description: Next/Previous buttons use Invoker Commands: commandfor + command='--workflow-next'/'--workflow-prev'.
invokerNavigation.rationale: Declarative navigation without JS event listeners. Consistent with platform direction (Invoker Commands Baseline 2026).

progressiveWizard.description: withBranchingLogic supports dynamic step lists that grow/shrink based on user input.
progressiveWizard.rationale: Many real-world workflows have conditional steps (e.g., 'Do you have dependents?' → show dependent steps). PatternFly's progressive wizard pattern addresses this.

name: Transient Component
summary: Base class and utilities for custom elements that replace themselves with semantically correct native HTML during connectedCallback — enabling context-aware element selection without permanent wrapper nodes.

replaceWith.pattern: Element.replaceWith() replaces an element in its parent's child list with new nodes
replaceWith.adoption: Core mechanism — transient element calls queueMicrotask(() => this.replaceWith(replacement)) from connectedCallback to safely self-replace after callback stack completes

customElements.pattern: Autonomous custom elements with connectedCallback lifecycle
customElements.adoption: Transient elements are autonomous custom elements that run initialization logic in connectedCallback, then replace themselves with native elements

queueMicrotask.pattern: Schedules a microtask to run after current script completes but before rendering
queueMicrotask.adoption: Defers replaceWith() out of connectedCallback stack to avoid re-entrancy with disconnectedCallback

headingElements.pattern: <h1>–<h6> provide document heading hierarchy; WHATWG outline algorithm removed from spec
headingElements.adoption: AutoHeading replaces itself with the correct <hN> element based on nesting depth or injector-provided level

ariaHeadingRole.pattern: role='heading' with aria-level for non-native heading semantics
ariaHeadingRole.adoption: Fallback only — MDN recommends native <hN> elements over role='heading'. Self-replacement produces native elements.

microtaskDeferral.description: Self-replacement uses queueMicrotask() to defer replaceWith() outside the connectedCallback stack
microtaskDeferral.rationale: Calling replaceWith() synchronously inside connectedCallback triggers disconnectedCallback re-entrantly. The microtask runs after connectedCallback completes but before paint — zero visual flash, clean lifecycle.

attributeTransfer.description: All attributes except internal ones (is, data-transient-*) are transferred to the replacement element
attributeTransfer.rationale: Authors expect class, id, style, aria-* and custom attributes to carry over. The transient element is a stand-in, not a wrapper.

childNodeTransfer.description: All child nodes are moved to the replacement element via append(...this.childNodes)
childNodeTransfer.rationale: Children belong to the semantic target, not the transient host. Moving (not cloning) preserves event listeners on children.

eventListenerWarning.description: Event listeners attached to the transient element itself are lost on replacement. This is by design.
eventListenerWarning.rationale: Transient elements are meant to be replaced before user interaction. Listeners should be on children (which survive) or on the replacement (added post-replacement via event delegation).

noCustomizedBuiltins.description: Does NOT use customized built-in elements (is='' attribute / extends: 'h1')
noCustomizedBuiltins.rationale: Safari refuses to implement customized built-ins and has stated it never will. The polyfill is unreliable. Autonomous custom elements that self-replace are the portable alternative.

injectorHeadingLevel.description: AutoHeading resolves heading level from InjectorRoot ('headingLevel' provider) first, falls back to DOM traversal (counting sectioning ancestors)
injectorHeadingLevel.rationale: InjectorRoot mirrors React's HeadingLevelContext pattern — a section component increments the level in its injector scope. All descendants read the correct level without DOM traversal. Fallback to DOM traversal covers cases without explicit section contexts.

ssrStrategy.description: SSR must compute the correct element server-side (e.g., Eleventy shortcode). Client-side transient element upgrades the fallback.
ssrStrategy.rationale: Custom elements require JS to connectedCallback. Crawlers and screen readers see the pre-upgrade HTML. The server must emit the correct <hN> for accessibility and SEO. The client-side element is progressive enhancement.

react.notes: React's context-based heading level is the canonical reference; components render the correct JSX element directly

angular.notes: ng-container removes itself at compile time — different mechanism but same goal (no wrapper node)

vue.notes: Dynamic component tag selection; template element renders no DOM

svelte.notes: Dynamic element tag at compile time; no runtime wrapper node

traits.name: withSelfReplacement
traits.description: Base mixin that handles the microtask-deferred replaceWith() lifecycle, attribute transfer, and child node migration
